decisionStatement: The Booking Engine is fit to become the local-first foundation for Phase 5, but not for an unconditional Phase 4 approval. The decisive 307 to 309 suites prove local booking truth, manage and waitlist behaviour, same-shell continuity, accessibility, and artifact parity; the exit remains constrained because performance follow-up is open and the release-scoped safety-delta and rollback-rehearsal artifacts are not present as machine-auditable evidence.
allowedNextSteps: Proceed to seq_311 through seq_313 to freeze the network coordination, policy, and cross-organisation commit contracts from the proven local-first booking baseline.
Proceed to seq_314 only if the readiness registry inherits the Phase 4 safety, rollback, and performance constraints instead of hiding them.
Treat BookingCase truth, fallback lineage, artifact law, and same-shell continuity as stable carry-forward inputs for Phase 5 work.
withheldClaims: Do not claim an unconditional Phase 4 approval for widened rollout or pilot widening.
Do not claim that sandbox capability evidence or unsupported edges are equivalent to live multi-provider parity.
Do not claim that the required release-scoped hazard-log delta or rollback rehearsal is complete for this candidate.

constraints.summary: Phase 4 release safety delta is not published as a release-specific artifact
constraints.nextAction: Carry the missing safety delta into the Phase 5 readiness gate, keep widened rollout withheld, and require a release-scoped hazard delta and safety case refresh before any later exit gate clears it.

constraints.summary: Rollback rehearsal evidence is absent from the Phase 4 exit candidate
constraints.nextAction: Keep rollout posture withheld, bind rollback readiness explicitly into the Phase 5 track gate, and require a captured rehearsal artifact before a future exit gate calls the booking engine unconditionally approved.

constraints.summary: Local booking load probe misses the 200ms interaction support target
constraints.nextAction: Propagate the performance constraint into the Phase 5 readiness registry, route UI and responsive remediation to the Phase 5 shell tracks, and require the later regression suites to prove the interaction target before widening.

constraints.summary: Live, sandbox, unsupported, and future-network provider claims must remain separated
constraints.nextAction: Carry the unsupported and sandbox-bound edges into the Phase 5 commit and visibility freeze pack so later network work expands capability truth without collapsing evidence classes.

mandatoryQuestions.question: Are the decisive evidence bundles from seq_307 to seq_309 present, fresh, and internally coherent?
mandatoryQuestions.answer: Yes. The repository contains fresh same-day machine-readable outputs for 307, 308, and 309, and their browser, service, accessibility, and artifact proof families are internally coherent.

mandatoryQuestions.question: Is the local booking engine itself proven for deterministic search, reservation, commit, manage, waitlist, assisted booking, reconciliation, and artifact truth?
mandatoryQuestions.answer: Yes for current repository truth. The local-first booking engine is implemented and proven across the full 307 to 309 battery for patient and staff flows.

mandatoryQuestions.question: Are live, sandbox, unsupported, and future-network claims clearly separated?
mandatoryQuestions.answer: Mostly. The gate now separates them explicitly, but the underlying evidence still includes sandbox-bound provider proof and an unsupported manual-assist edge that must remain visibly constrained.

mandatoryQuestions.question: Can the Phase 4 exit be called `approved` with the current evidence candidate?
mandatoryQuestions.answer: No. The performance support target is currently missed and the release-scoped safety-delta and rollback-rehearsal artifacts are absent, so an unconditional approval would overstate repository truth.

mandatoryQuestions.question: Is the booking engine fit to become the local-first foundation for the network horizon?
mandatoryQuestions.answer: Yes, with explicit constraints. Phase 5 may start from this foundation only if it inherits the performance, safety, rollback, and provider-boundary constraints without relabeling them as solved.

rows.summary: Capability resolution, slot snapshots, reservation truth, stale-slot fencing, double-book resistance, and commit replay law are all proven in the current repository for the local-first booking engine.

rows.capabilityFamily: Provider capability boundary, unsupported paths, and evidence-class honesty
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4B. Provider capability matrix and adapter seam
/Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#Tests that must all pass before Phase 5
rows.summary: The booking engine correctly enforces capability law and exposes unsupported supplier paths as unsupported instead of silently widening them, but the stronger provider evidence remains sandbox-bound and one manual-assist network path is still explicitly unsupported.
rows.blockingRationale: This is sufficient for local-first foundation truth, but not for live or cross-organisation provider parity claims.

rows.capabilityFamily: Ambiguous confirmation, compensation, and recovery truth
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4E. Commit path, confirmation truth, and failure recovery
rows.summary: Ambiguous commit reconciliation, duplicate callback handling, compensation, dispute recovery, and cross-surface truth alignment are all proven with executable browser and service evidence.

rows.capabilityFamily: Manage commands, waitlist continuation, assisted booking, and reconciliation parity
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4F. Manage flows, waitlist, and assisted booking
rows.summary: Patient manage flows, waitlist offer and fallback law, staff-assisted booking, review-action lease behaviour, and reconciliation status parity are all proven and consistent across domain and browser suites.

rows.capabilityFamily: Patient and staff shell continuity, route publication, and embedded parity
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#Tests that must all pass before Phase 5
rows.summary: Patient and staff booking routes now prove same-shell continuity, route-contract and recovery-disposition behaviour, responsive parity, and embedded-mode freeze-safe behaviour for the local-first surface family.

rows.capabilityFamily: Artifact presentation, print-export parity, and outbound navigation policy
rows.summary: Appointment summaries, confirmation artifacts, print/export states, browser handoff, and recovery-only artifact posture are all represented accurately and proven in browser-visible evidence.

rows.capabilityFamily: Accessibility, keyboard flow, reduced motion, and visual parity
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#Tests that must all pass before Phase 5
rows.summary: The booking surface family has passing accessibility matrices, ARIA snapshots, reduced-motion parity, visual-regression proof, and mobile through desktop evidence across patient and staff contexts.

rows.capabilityFamily: Lifecycle, reminders, notifications, and quiet re-entry
rows.summary: The repository proves lifecycle and notification truth for local booking entry, reopen-safe re-entry, record-origin continuity, and reminder-adjacent artifact states without contradicting the Phase 4 reminder scheduler boundary.

rows.capabilityFamily: Performance budgets and release-watch support posture
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#Tests that must all pass before Phase 5
rows.summary: The release-grade browser suite passed and the evidence bundle is fresh, but the local booking load probe exceeded the interaction support target in all realistic scenarios, so widened rollout posture must stay constrained.
rows.blockingRationale: The load probe recorded p75 interaction times of 334.42ms, 744.8ms, and 1648.76ms against the 200ms support target.

rows.capabilityFamily: Release safety evidence and rollback rehearsal completeness
sourceSections: /Users/test/Code/V/blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#Tests that must all pass before Phase 5
rows.summary: The candidate shows mature booking truth and browser parity, but the required Phase 4 release-scoped hazard-log delta and rollback rehearsal artifacts are not present as machine-auditable exit evidence in the current repository.
rows.blockingRationale: These evidence families are mandatory in the Phase 4 algorithm. Their absence blocks an `approved` exit and blocks any widened rollout claim.

boundaryItems.title: Freeze hub case, acting context, and organisation-boundary law from Phase 4 fallback truth
boundaryItems.risk: Without a frozen handoff contract, Phase 5 could reinterpret local fallback and lose the exact BookingCase lineage that 307 to 309 proved.
boundaryItems.nextAction: Carry forward BookingCase lineage, fallback reasons, and same-shell continuity rules into the HubCoordinationCase freeze pack.

boundaryItems.title: Freeze policy tuple, capacity trust, and candidate-ranking formulas
boundaryItems.risk: Network candidate ranking could drift away from the deterministic local-first ordering and capability evidence that Phase 4 proved.
boundaryItems.nextAction: Bind robust-fit, uncertainty, and source-trust formulas to one policy tuple without loosening Phase 4 proof law.

boundaryItems.title: Freeze cross-organisation commit, practice visibility, and manage-capability boundaries
boundaryItems.risk: Unsupported or sandbox-only provider paths could be mislabeled as confirmed network truth if commit and visibility contracts are not frozen next.
boundaryItems.nextAction: Publish the cross-org confirmation and visibility pack with explicit evidence-class separation and minimum-necessary practice visibility.

boundaryItems.title: Launch Phase 5 only with inherited Phase 4 constraints still live in the readiness registry
boundaryItems.risk: Performance, safety, and rollback gaps could disappear into prose unless the first network gate inherits them as named blockers.
boundaryItems.nextAction: Mark safety delta, rollback rehearsal, and performance budget follow-up as explicit readiness rows before opening downstream tracks.

boundaryItems.title: Implement the hub case kernel without redefining booking lineage, rollback posture, or recovery semantics
boundaryItems.risk: Phase 5 backend work could bypass the proven BookingCase recovery and lineage model if the first hub kernel is implemented loosely.
boundaryItems.nextAction: Implement HubCoordinationCase as a faithful consumer of Phase 4 fallback and recovery truth, not as a detached rewrite.

boundaryItems.title: Expand provider commit and practice continuity only after Phase 4 unsupported edges stay explicit
boundaryItems.risk: Cross-org commit work could overstate supplier and practice confirmation certainty if unsupported Phase 4 edges are forgotten.
boundaryItems.nextAction: Preserve the unsupported and sandbox-only boundaries when native hub commit and practice continuity messaging land.
